{{startCommand}}

Please report this information to the administrator, technician or troubleshoot yourself.

TXT_CODE_docker_version_select_desc: This template supports both standard and Docker modes. Choosing Docker mode will override with Docker-recommended settings.
TXT_CODE_docker_version_select_docker_su: Docker deployment requires installing Docker, but you do not need to install runtime environments on the host. It is the preferred choice for Linux systems.
TXT_CODE_docker_version_select_normal_su: Standard installation runs directly on the host. You need to install runtime environments yourself, such as Java or Python.

TXT_CODE_f94734d8: Insufficient storage space. Your storage quota is {{storageLimit}}GB, currently used {{storageUsage}}GB

TXT_CODE_d86fbff5: This limit is only supported on Linux systems. It relies on the system command `du`. Before startup and during runtime, the working directory size is checked every 1 to 10 minutes. If the limit is exceeded, the instance will be automatically stopped.
TXT_CODE_285cef0c: Unit: GB. For example, 60 means 60GB, 0 means unlimited.
TXT_CODE_network_upload_limit_help: Set container upload bandwidth limit, 0 means unlimited
TXT_CODE_network_download_limit_help: Set container download bandwidth limit, 0 means unlimited
TXT_CODE_SSO_ENABLE_DESC: Select a third-party login protocol. OIDC supports auto-discovery; OAuth 2.0 requires manual endpoint configuration (e.g. GitHub).
TXT_CODE_SSO_ENABLE_REQUIRES_CONFIG: Please complete the required SSO configuration before enabling.
TXT_CODE_SSO_OAUTH2_REQUIRES_URLS: OAuth 2.0 mode requires Authorize URL, Token URL, and Userinfo URL.
TXT_CODE_SSO_ONLY_MODE_DESC: When enabled, password login will be disabled and only SSO login is allowed.
TXT_CODE_SSO_AUTO_REDIRECT_DESC: When enabled, visiting the login page will automatically redirect to the SSO provider. Append ?ssoAutoRedirect=false to the login URL to temporarily disable.
TXT_CODE_SSO_PROVIDER_NAME_DESC: Display name shown on the login page SSO button.
TXT_CODE_SSO_ICON_URL_DESC: Icon URL for the SSO provider, displayed on the login button.
TXT_CODE_SSO_ISSUER_DESC: OIDC Issuer URL (without /.well-known/openid-configuration). Used for service discovery.
TXT_CODE_SSO_AUTHORIZE_URL_DESC: OAuth 2.0 authorization endpoint. Users will be redirected here to log in.
TXT_CODE_SSO_TOKEN_URL_DESC: OAuth 2.0 token endpoint. Used to exchange authorization code for access token.
TXT_CODE_SSO_USERINFO_URL_DESC: OAuth 2.0 userinfo endpoint. Used to fetch user identity with access token.
TXT_CODE_SSO_USER_ID_FIELD_DESC: Field name in userinfo response to use as unique user identifier. GitHub uses 'id', Google uses 'sub'.
TXT_CODE_SSO_SCOPES_DESC: Custom OAuth 2.0 scopes. Leave empty for default (OAuth 2.0: read:user).
TXT_CODE_SSO_CLIENT_ID_DESC: Client ID provided by the identity provider.
TXT_CODE_SSO_CLIENT_SECRET_DESC: Client Secret provided by the identity provider.
TXT_CODE_SSO_CALLBACK_URL_DESC: OAuth2 redirect URI. If empty, it will be auto-generated based on panel address. Must match the redirect URI configured in the IdP.
TXT_CODE_SSO_IDENTITY_CHANGE_CONFIRM: You are changing critical SSO identity settings (protocol type, Issuer URL, Userinfo URL, or User ID field). After saving, all existing SSO-bound users will be unbound and will need to re-link their third-party accounts. Continue?
TXT_CODE_SSO_BIND_DESC: First time using SSO login. Please bind your CI Panel account.
TXT_CODE_SSO_BIND_CURRENT_HINT: You are currently logged in as the following account. You can bind directly:
TXT_CODE_SSO_UNBIND_CONFIRM: Are you sure to unbind SSO for user {userName}?
TXT_CODE_SSO_ONLY_MODE_WARN: Password login is disabled. Please use SSO login.
TXT_CODE_bdb9f7bb: Network rate limiting failed to enable! Please ensure the control panel can read system commands or network adapter information, such as tc, ip, modprobe, ifb, etc., or choose to disable network rate limiting for this instance in the settings.
TXT_CODE_49731eec: Network rate limiting failed to enable! Please ensure the control panel can read container information or network adapter information, or choose to disable network rate limiting for this instance in the settings.
TXT_CODE_gpu_enable_help: Enable GPU passthrough for this Docker container. Requires NVIDIA Container Toolkit installed on the host.
TXT_CODE_gpu_driver_help: GPU driver name, typically nvidia. Only letters and digits are allowed.
TXT_CODE_gpu_alloc_mode_help: Choose how to allocate GPUs: all available GPUs, a specific count, or specific device IDs. These options are mutually exclusive.
TXT_CODE_gpu_count_help: Number of GPUs to allocate, must be a positive integer.
TXT_CODE_gpu_device_ids_help: Comma-separated GPU device IDs. Each ID may only contain letters, digits, hyphens, and underscores. e.g., 0,1 or GPU-xxxx
TXT_CODE_gpu_invalid_count: Invalid GPU count: {{v}}. Must be an integer >= -1.
TXT_CODE_gpu_invalid_device_id: Invalid GPU device ID: {{v}}. Only letters, digits, hyphens and underscores are allowed.
TXT_CODE_gpu_invalid_driver: Invalid GPU driver name: {{v}}. Only letters and digits are allowed.
TXT_CODE_gpu_conflict_count_and_ids: GPU configuration conflict: cannot specify both GPU count and device IDs at the same time.
TXT_CODE_f18f65db: Please make sure your current work has been saved. You can freely adjust the interface after entering. The page will refresh after exiting!
TXT_CODE_2b85af6d: If you are accessing the panel through a reverse proxy, please enable this option. The panel will retrieve your real IP address from a specific request header.
TXT_CODE_fd8bc51f: When reverse proxy mode is enabled, the panel will retrieve the user's real IP address from this request header.
TXT_CODE_upload_folder_too_large: The folder is too large (over {size}), please compress it manually and then upload it.
TXT_CODE_2bda9d65: A download request already exists at this location. Please wait for it to complete before starting a new download.
TXT_CODE_gzip_compress_desc: Compress HTTP responses (zstd, gzip, etc., auto-negotiated). Responses under 1 KB are skipped.
TXT_CODE_edbb92dc: Limit the read rate of specific block devices for the container. Format: /dev/sda:10MB. Separate multiple devices with commas.
TXT_CODE_2b2ff3ff: Limit the write rate of specific block devices for the container. Format: /dev/sda:5MB. Separate multiple devices with commas.
